title: Step 7: Add the new database user or group.

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To manage Workforce Identity Federation configuration, you must have
[Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) access to Atlas.

sections.heading: Procedures
sections.body:
To access Atlas clusters with Workforce Identity Federation, complete the following steps:

- Configure a Workforce Identity Provider (one-time setup).

  - [Configure An External Identity Provider Application](configure-idp-oidc).

  - [enable Workload IdP for your organization](configure-oidc).

- Grant external identities (user principals) or groups access
  to MongoDB clusters.

- Authenticate to your Atlas clusters with MongoDB Shell or Compass.

sections.heading: Configure An External Identity Provider Application
sections.body:
To configure Workforce Identity Federation with OIDC (OpenID Connect), register your OIDC (OpenID Connect)
application with an IdP (Identity Provider) that supports OIDC (OpenID Connect) standard, such
as [Microsoft Entra ID](configure-azure-idp-oidc), [Okta](configure-okta-idp-oidc), or
Ping Identity.

> **Note:** Workforce Identity Federation supports only JWT for authentication. It doesn't
> support opaque access tokens.

You can configure your OIDC (OpenID Connect) application for the following grant types:

- Authorization Code Flow with PKCE (Proof Key of Code Exchange)

- Device Authorization Flow

MongoDB recommends that you use Authorization Code Flow with PKCE (Proof Key of Code Exchange) for increased
security. Use Device Authorization Flow only if your users need
to access the database from machines with no browser.

The OIDC (OpenID Connect) application registration steps can vary based on your
IdP (Identity Provider). Ensure that you complete the following items during
your registration process:

sections.heading: Step 1: Register a new application for .
sections.body: Select **public client/native application** as the client type.

sections.heading: Step 2: Set the  value to .
Step 3: (Conditional) Add or enable a  claim if you authenticate with groups.
sections.body:
For groups, this step ensures that your access tokens contain the group
membership information of the user authenticating. MongoDB uses the
values sent in a groups claim for authorization.

sections.heading: Step 4
sections.body:
(Optional) Allow refresh tokens if you want MongoDB clients to
refresh the tokens for a better user experience.

sections.heading: Step 5
sections.body:
(Optional) Configure the access token lifetime (`exp` claim) to align with
your database connection session time.

After you register your application, save the `issuer`,
`clientId`, and `audience` values to use in the next stage of the
[Atlas Workforce IdP configuration](apply-oidc-idp).

Microsoft Entra ID

Okta

sections.heading: Step 5
sections.body:
To register your OIDC (OpenID Connect) application with Microsoft Entra ID:

- Navigate to App registrations.

  - In your [Azure portal](https://portal.azure.com/) account, search and click Microsoft Entra ID.

  - In the Manage section of the left navigation, click App registrations.

- Click New registration.

- Apply the following values.

  | Field                   | Value                                                                       |
  | ----------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
  | Name                    | Atlas Database - Workforce                                                  |
  | Supported Account Types | Accounts in this organizational directory only (single tenant)              |
  | Redirect URI            | - Public client/native (mobile & desktop)- http\://localhost:27097/redirect |

- Click Register.

To learn more about registering an application, see [Azure Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/develop/quickstart-register-app#register-an-application).

- Navigate to Token Configuration.

  In the Manage section of the left navigation,
  click Token Configuration.

- Click Add groups claim.

- In the Edit groups claim modal, select Security groups.

  What groups you select depend on the type of groups you configured
  in your Azure environment. You may need to select a different
  type of group to send the appropriate group information.

- In the Customize token properties by type section, only select Group ID.

- Click Add.

To learn more about adding a group claim, see [Azure Documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/hybrid/connect/how-to-connect-fed-group-claims).

- Click Add optional claim.

- In the Add optional claim modal, select Access.

- Select a claim that carries a user identifier that you can
  refer to in MongoDB access logs such as an email.

  You can use the UPN (UserPrincipalName) claim to identify users with their email address.

- Click Add.

- In the Microsoft Graph Permissions note, check the box, and click Add.

To learn more, see [Azure Documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/develop/optional-claims).

- In the Manage section of the left navigation, click Manifest.

- Update the requestedAccessTokenVersion from `null` to `2`.

  The number `2` represents Version 2 of Microsoft's access
  tokens. Other applications can use this as a signed
  attestation of the Active Directory-managed user's identity.
  Version 2 ensures that the token is a JSON Web Token that
  MongoDB understands.

- Click Save.

To learn more about adding an optional claim, see [Azure Documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/develop/reference-app-manifest).

- In the left navigation, click Overview.

  Copy the Application (client) ID value.

- In the top navigation, click Endpoints.

  Copy the OpenID Connect metadata document value
  without the `/.well-known/openid-configuration` part.

  You can also get this value by copying the value for
  `issuer` in the OpenID Connect metadata document URL (Uniform Resource Locator).

The following table shows what these Microsoft Entra ID UI values map to in our Atlas Configuration Properties:

| Microsoft Entra ID UI                                                        | Atlas Configuration Property |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- |
| Application (client) ID                                                      | Client IDAudience            |
| OpenID Connect metadata document (without /.well-known/openid-configuration) | Issuer URI                   |

sections.heading: Step 5
sections.body:
To register your OIDC (OpenID Connect) application with Okta:

In your Okta Admin dashboard, use the left navigation pane
to go to Applications → Applications.

- On the Applications screen, click Create App
  Integration.

- In the Sign-in method section, select OIDC - OpenID Connect.

- In the Application type section, select Native Application.

- Click Next.

To learn more, see [Create OIDC app Integrations](https://help.okta.com/en-us/content/topics/apps/apps_app_integration_wizard_oidc.htm).

After you create an app integration, you are automatically redirected to the
New Native App Integration screen.

- In the App integration name field, enter a name for your
  application.

- In the Grant type field, select grant types.

  Enable the following grant types:

  - Authorization Code or Device Authorization

  - *(Optional)* Refresh Token

    Enabling refresh tokens provides a better user experience. When
    refresh tokens are not enabled, users must re-authenticate with
    the identity provider once their access token expires.

- In the Sign-in redirect URIs section, enter a URL.

  Enter the following URL: `http://localhost:27097/redirect`.

- In the Assignments section, configure the Controlled access
  and Enable immediate access fields.

  - For the Controlled access field, select Allow everyone
    in your organization to access.

  - For Enable immediate access field, ensure Enable immediate
    access with Federation Broker Mode is checked.

- Click Save.

To learn more, see [Create OIDC app integrations](https://help.okta.com/en-us/content/topics/apps/apps_app_integration_wizard_oidc.htm).

On your application dashboard, go to the General tab
and configure the following:

- In the Client ID field, click the 
  icon to copy the client ID for later use.

- In the Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) field, ensure
  Require PKCE as additional verification is enabled (checked
  by default).

In the left navigation pane, go to Security → API. Click
Add Authorization Server.

- In the Name field, enter a name for your server.

- In the Audience field, paste the client ID from the previous
  step.

- *(Optional)* In the Description field, enter a description of
  your server.

- Click Save.

To learn more, see [Create an Authorization Server](https://help.okta.com/en-us/content/topics/security/api-config-auth-server.htm).

After you create your authorization server, you are automatically
redirected to your authorization server's screen.

Under the Settings tab, save the issuer URI by copying the
first part of the Metadata URI up to the `.well-known`
section. The URI structure should be similar to:
`https://trial4238026.okta.com/oauth2/ausabgmhveoOQSMsE697`.

On your authorization server screen, go to the Claims tab and
click Add Claim.

- Configure Groups claim with the following configuration information:

  | Field                 | Value                                                                           |
  | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
  | Name                  | Enter a name for your claim.                                                    |
  | Include in token type | Click the drop-down and select Access Token.                                    |
  | Value type            | Click the drop-down and select Groups.                                          |
  | Filter                | Click the drop-down and select Matches regex. Next to the drop-down, enter .\*. |
  | Disable claim         | Do not check.                                                                   |
  | Include in            | Select Any scope.                                                               |

- Click Create.

To learn more, see [Create Claims](https://help.okta.com/en-us/content/topics/security/api-config-claims.htm).

On your authorization server screen, go to the Access Policies
tab and click Add Policy.

- In the Name field, enter a policy name.

- In the Description field, enter a description for the policy.

- In the Assign to field, select All clients.

- Click Create Policy.

To learn more, see [Create an Access Policy](https://help.okta.com/en-us/content/topics/security/api-config-access-policies.htm).

Under the Access Policies tab, click Add Rule.

- In the Rule Name field, enter a name for the access policy.

- For IF Grant Type is, select a grant type.

  When configuring grant types, select the appropriate option based on the
  client behavior:

  - If the client is acting on behalf of itself, select Client
    Credentials.

  - If the client is acting on behalf of a user, select the following:

    - Authorization Code

    - Device Authorization

- Add rule configurations based on your organization's security policy.

  Example Okta rule configuration:

  | Field                             | Value                                            |
  | --------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
  | AND user is                       | Select Any user assigned to the app.             |
  | AND Scopes requested              | Select Any scopes.                               |
  | THEN Use this inline hook         | None (disabled)                                  |
  | AND Access token lifetime is      | 1 Hours                                          |
  | AND Refresh token lifetime is     | Click the second drop-down and select Unlimited. |
  | but will expire if not used every | Enter 7 days.                                    |

- Click Create Rule.

To learn more, see [Create Rules for each Access Policy](https://developer.okta.com/docs/guides/customize-authz-server/main/#create-rules-for-each-access-policy).

In the left navigation pane, go to Directory → Groups and click
Add Group.

- In the Name field, name your directory `OIDC`.

- *(Optional)* In the Description field, enter a description
  for your rule.

- Click Save.

  To learn more, see [Create a Group](https://help.okta.com/en-us/content/topics/users-groups-profiles/usgp-groups-create.htm).

- Follow the Okta documentation to
  [manually assign people to the group](https://help.okta.com/en-us/content/topics/users-groups-profiles/usgp-assign-group-people.htm).

In the left navigation pane, go to Directory → People and click
Add Person.

- Provide user details by entering the following values in the
  corresponding fields:

  | Field                                    | Value                                                                          |
  | ---------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
  | User type                                | Select User.                                                                   |
  | First name                               | Provide name as needed.                                                        |
  | Last name                                | Provide name as needed.                                                        |
  | Username                                 | Enter an email as a username.                                                  |
  | Primary email                            | Enter an email. The email must be same as the one used for the Username field. |
  | Secondary email                          | Optional.                                                                      |
  | Groups                                   | Enter OIDC.                                                                    |
  | Activation                               | Select Activate Now and check I will set password.                             |
  | Password                                 | Enter a password.                                                              |
  | User must change password on first login | Select Optional                                                                |

- Click Save.

To learn more, see [Add Users Manually](https://help.okta.com/en-us/content/topics/users-groups-profiles/usgp-add-users.htm).

sections.heading: Configure Workforce Identity Federation Authentication
sections.body:
> **Note:** 
>
> Prerequisite
>
> This procedure requires you to have [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>)
> access and assumes you already have an OIDC (OpenID Connect) application
> created in your IdP (Identity Provider). To learn
> how to configure an IdP (Identity Provider), see [Configure An External Identity Provider Application](configure-idp-oidc).

To configure a Workforce Identity Provider in Atlas:

sections.heading: Step 1: Configure identify providers.
sections.body:
- Click **Identity Providers** in the left sidebar.

- Do one of the following steps:

  - If you do not have any Identity Providers configured yet, click
    **Set Up Identity Provider**.

  - Otherwise, on the **Identity Providers** screen, click
    **Configure Identity Provider(s)**.

- Select **Workforce Identity Federation** and click **Continue**.

- Select **OIDC for Data Access**.

sections.heading: Step 2: Enter the following Workforce Identity Provider Protocol Settings.
sections.body:
| Setting                   | Necessity | Value                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ------------------------- | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Configuration Name        | Required  | Human-readable label that identifies this configuration. This label is visible to your Atlas users.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| Configuration Description | Optional  | Human-readable label that describes this configuration.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| Issuer URI                | Required  | Issuer value provided by your registered IdP (Identity Provider) application. Using this URI, MongoDB finds an OpenID Provider Configuration Document, which should be available in the /.wellknown/open-id-configuration endpoint.                                                                                                                                                       |
| Client ID                 | Required  | Unique identifier for your registered application. Enter the clientId value from the app you registered with external Identity Provider.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Audience                  | Required  | Entity that your external identity provider intends the token for. Enter the audience value from the app you registered with external Identity Provider. Generally, this value is the same as the Client ID.                                                                                                                                                                              |
| Requested Scopes          | Optional  | Tokens that give users permission to request data from the authorization endpoint. If you plan to support refresh tokens, this field must include the value offline\_access.If your identity provider is Microsoft Entra ID, Atlas requires this setting. Add default scope, which is \<application client id>/.default.For each additional scope you want to add, click Add more scopes. |
| Authorization Type        | Required  | Select Group Membership to grant authorization based on IdP (Identity Provider) user group membership, or select User ID to grant an individual user authorization.                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| Customize Groups Claim    | Required  | Identifier of the claim that includes the principal's IdP (Identity Provider) user group membership information. Accept the default value unless your IdP (Identity Provider) uses a different claim, or you need a custom claim. This field is only required if you select Group Membership.Default: groups                                                                              |
| Customize User Claim      | Required  | Identifier of the claim that includes the user principal identity. Accept the default value unless your IdP (Identity Provider) uses a different claim.Default: sub                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |

sections.heading: Step 3: Click .
Step 4: (Optional) Associate a domain to your Workforce .
sections.body:
> **Note:** This step is required only if you need to connect multiple Workforce IdP (Identity Provider)s
> to the same organization with different domains. Atlas supports a
> maximum of two Workforce IdP (Identity Provider)s connected to an organization: one OIDC IdP (Identity Provider)
> (for database access) and one SAML IdP (Identity Provider) (for UI access).

- In your Workforce Identity Provider card, click **Associate Domains**.

- In the **Associate Domains with Identity Provider** modal,
  select one or more domains.

- Click **Submit**.

sections.heading: Step 5: Enable your Workforce  in an organization.
sections.body:
- Click **Connect Organizations**.

- For the organization you want to connect to Workforce Identity Provider,
  click **Configure Access**.

- Click **Connect Identity Provider**.

  > **Note:** If you have another IdP (Identity Provider) configured, this button says
  > **Connect Identity Provider(s)**.

sections.heading: Step 6: Select .
sections.body:
In the **Connect Identity Provider(s)** modal, select  a
Workforce Identity Provider where the **Purpose** is
**Workforce Identity Federation**.

sections.heading: Step 7: Click .
sections.body:
When you connect your Workforce Identity Provider to an organization, Atlas
enables your Workforce Identity Provider for all the projects within that organization.

sections.heading: Add a Database User using Workforce Authentication
sections.body:
Click  Add New Database User or Group.

> **Note:** Until you [Atlas Workforce IdP configuration](apply-oidc-idp), this button says 
> Add New Database User.

In the Authentication Method section, select Federated Auth.

> **Note:** Until you [enable Workload IdP for your organization](configure-oidc),
> you can't select this box.

a. In the Select Identity Provider section, select a configured
OIDC (OpenID Connect) Identity Provider.

- Specify either the user identifier or group identifier associated with
  your configured Workforce Identity Provider.

> **Note:** For Azure Entra ID users, this value maps to the Object Id of your
> Azure user group rather than user group name.

To assign privileges to the new user or group, do one or more of the
following tasks:

- Select a [built-in role](manual/reference/built-in-roles) from the
  Built-in Role dropdown menu.

  - You can select one built-in role per database group in the Atlas UI.

  - If you delete the default option, you can click Add Built-in Role
    to select a new built-in role.

- Select or add [custom roles](mongodb-roles).

  - If you have any custom roles defined, you can expand
    the Custom Roles section and select
    one or more roles from the Custom Roles dropdown menu.

  - Click Add Custom Role to add more custom roles.

  - Click the Custom Roles link to see the custom
    roles for your project.

- Add [privileges](manual/reference/built-in-roles).

  - Expand the Specific Privileges section and select one or more
    privileges from the Specific Privileges dropdown menu.

  - Click Add Specific Privilege to add more privileges. This assigns
    the group specific privileges on individual databases and collections.

- Remove an applied role or privilege.

  - Click 

     

    Delete

     next to the

    role or privilege to delete.

  > **Note:** Atlas doesn't display the  Delete icon
  > next to your Built-in Role, Custom Role, or
  > Specific Privilege selection if you selected only one option. You
  > can delete the selected role or privilege once you apply another role or privilege.

Atlas can apply a built-in role, multiple custom roles, and multiple specific
privileges to a database group.

To learn more about authorization, see [Role-Based
Access Control](manual/core/authorization) and [Built-in
Roles](manual/reference/built-in-roles) in the MongoDB manual.

By default, groups can access all the clusters and federated database instances in the
project. To restrict access to specific clusters and federated database instances:

- Toggle Restrict Access to Specific Clusters/Federated
  Database Instances to On.

- Select the clusters and federated database instances to grant the group access to
  from the Grant Access To list.

Toggle Temporary User or Temporary Group to
On and choose a time after which Atlas can delete the user
or group from the Temporary User Duration or
Temporary Group Duration dropdown. You can select one of the
following time periods for the group to exist:

- 6 hours

- 1 day

- 1 week

In the Database Users tab, temporary users or groups display
the time remaining until Atlas deletes the users or group. After
Atlas deletes the user or group, any client or application that uses
the temporary user's or group's credentials loses access to the cluster.

Do one of the following steps:

- If you added a user, click the Add User button.

- If you added a group, click the Add Group button.

sections.heading: Connect a Client to MongoDB with Workforce Identity Federation
sections.body:
The following lists the ways you can connect a client to MongoDB
with Workforce Identity Federation authentication:

- [Compass](atlas-connect-via-compass) v1.38+

- [MongoDB Shell](connect-mongo-shell) v2.1.4+

sections.heading: Manage an Existing Workforce Identity Federation Configuration
View Workforce Identity Provider Configuration
Step 1: Click  in the left sidebar.
sections.body: You can view all configured identity providers and their details.

sections.heading: Update Workforce Identity Provider Configuration
Step 1: Click  in the left sidebar.
Step 2: In the Workforce Identity Provider card, click the  dropdown, then .
Step 3: Make your changes and click .
View and Manage Connected Organizations
Step 1: Click  in the left sidebar.
sections.body: You can view all connected organizations and manage their configurations.

sections.heading: Revoke JWKS
sections.body:
> **Note:** Use the following procedure only if you manage your own signing keys.
>
> Don't use this feature to rotate your signing keys. When you rotate
> your Workforce Identity Provider signing keys, MongoDB fetches the JWKS automatically
> when the existing access tokens expire.

If your private key is compromised, you can immediately revoke your JSON
Web Key Sets (JWKS) cached in MongoDB nodes:

sections.heading: Step 1: Update your signing keys in your Workforce identity provider.
Step 2: Click  in the left sidebar.
Step 3: Scroll to the Workforce Identity Provider card.
Step 4: Click the  dropdown, then .
Step 5: Click .
sections.body:
After you click **Revoke**, MongoDB fetches the new keys
through your JWKS endpoint. You must restart your clients (such as
[mongosh](mongodb-shell) or [Compass](compass)) after you revoke JWKS.

sections.heading: Delete a Workforce Identity Provider Configuration
sections.body: To delete your Workforce Identity Provider configuration:

sections.heading: Step 1: Disconnect each organization you connected to your Workforce .
sections.body:
- Click **Organizations** in the left sidebar.

- Click the organization that has Workforce Identity Federation enabled.

- Click **Disconnect** under the **Manage** dropdown on the Workforce Identity Federation card.

- In the **Disconnect identity provider?** modal, click
  **Disconnect**.

  When you disconnect an IdP (Identity Provider), users who authenticate using the
  IdP (Identity Provider) lose access to Workforce Identity Federation in the Atlas projects
  listed in the **Project** table.

sections.heading: Step 2: Click  in the left sidebar.
Step 3: In the Workforce Identity Provider card, click the  dropdown, then .
Step 4
sections.body:
In the **Delete Identity Provider?** modal, click
**Delete**.

sections.heading: Manage Workforce Identity Federation with the Atlas CLI
sections.body:
You can use the Atlas CLI to manage Workforce Identity Federation configuration
for your organizations.

sections.heading: Configure Workforce Identity Federation Authentication
sections.body:
To create an OIDC identity provider using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings identityProvider create oidc [displayName] [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings identityProvider create oidc](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-federatedAuthentication-federationSettings-identityProvider-create-oidc).

codeBlocks.content: atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings identityProvider create oidc [displayName] [options]

sections.heading: Enable Workforce Identity Provider in an Organization
sections.body:
To connect an identity provider to an organization using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings connectedOrgConfigs connect [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings connectedOrgConfigs connect](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-federatedAuthentication-federationSettings-connectedOrgConfigs-connect).

sections.heading: View Workforce Identity Provider Configuration
sections.body:
To list all identity providers:

To list the identity providers from your federation settings using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings identityProvider list [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings identityProvider list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-federatedAuthentication-federationSettings-identityProvider-list).

To view details of a specific identity provider:

To describe the specified identity provider from your federation settings using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings identityProvider describe <identityProviderId> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings identityProvider describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-federatedAuthentication-federationSettings-identityProvider-describe).

To view federation settings:

To return the federation settings details for the specified organization using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings describe [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-federatedAuthentication-federationSettings-describe).

sections.heading: Update Workforce Identity Provider Configuration
sections.body:
To update an OIDC identity provider using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings identityProvider update oidc [identityProviderId] [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings identityProvider update oidc](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-federatedAuthentication-federationSettings-identityProvider-update-oidc).

codeBlocks.content: atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings identityProvider update oidc [identityProviderId] [options]

sections.heading: View and Manage Connected Organizations
sections.body:
To list connected organizations:

To list connected org configs using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings connectedOrgConfigs list [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings connectedOrgConfigs list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-federatedAuthentication-federationSettings-connectedOrgConfigs-list).

To view details of a connected organization:

To describe a connected org config using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings connectedOrgConfigs describe [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings connectedOrgConfigs describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-federatedAuthentication-federationSettings-connectedOrgConfigs-describe).

To update a connected organization:

To update one org config connected to one federation setting using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings connectedOrgConfigs update [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings connectedOrgConfigs update](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-federatedAuthentication-federationSettings-connectedOrgConfigs-update).

To delete a connected organization:

To delete a connected org config organization using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings connectedOrgConfigs delete [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings connectedOrgConfigs delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-federatedAuthentication-federationSettings-connectedOrgConfigs-delete).

sections.heading: Revoke JWKS
sections.body:
To revoke the JWK token from the specified identity provider from your federation settings using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings identityProvider revokeJwk <identityProviderId> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings identityProvider revokeJwk](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-federatedAuthentication-federationSettings-identityProvider-revokeJwk).

sections.heading: Delete a Workforce Identity Provider Configuration
sections.body:
To disconnect an organization:

To disconnect an identity provider from an organization using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings connectedOrgConfigs disconnect [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings connectedOrgConfigs disconnect](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-federatedAuthentication-federationSettings-connectedOrgConfigs-disconnect).

To delete the identity provider:

To remove the specified identity provider from your federation settings using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings identityProvider delete <identityProviderId> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas federatedAuthentication federationSettings identityProvider delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-federatedAuthentication-federationSettings-identityProvider-delete).
